title: Tilray (TLRY) Q3 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
text: Cannabis company Tilray Brands will report quarterly earnings before the opening bell on Thursday. In the previous quarter, Tilray missed analysts' revenue expectations by 2% with revenues of $224.5 million, representing a 2.3% year-over-year decline. The company also disappointed on gross margin estimates and full-year EBITDA guidance. Analysts anticipate modest growth this quarter, projecting revenue to grow 2% year on year to $204 million, compared to a 13.1% increase in the same period last year. This represents a significant slowdown in growth momentum. Tilray has missed Wall Street's revenue estimates five times over the last two years, suggesting a pattern of underperformance relative to expectations. Notably, Tilray shares are up 51.8% over the past month, yet analysts maintain an average price target of $0.98 compared to the current share price of $1.70, indicating potential overvaluation concerns.
